title: Nebula-class Star Destroyer

The '''''Nebula''-class Star Destroyer''', also known as the '''''Defender''-class Star Destroyer''', was the largest, most powerful [[Warship/Legends|warship]] design in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[New Class Modernization Program]].<ref name="CTD" />

==Characteristics==
Although it was only 1,040 meters long (65% of the length of an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]]), the ''Nebula''-class was built to be a match for an [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II'']].<ref name="CTD" /> Even with a structure that was a significant departure from the ''Imperial''-class, especially the lack of a command tower, the ''Nebula'' was often mistaken for an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] design due to its angular lines.<ref name="SOTG'01" /> Its small size often lead to it being referred to as a "pocket [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]",<ref name="CTD" /> much as the later [[Turbulent-class Star Destroyer|''Turbulent''-class]] would be as well.<ref name="R">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>

It was designed to be able to defeat any one enemy [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], two heavy [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], or an entire line of smaller Imperial support ships.<ref name="CTD" /> It mounted defenses strong enough to resist even some of the smaller Super Star Destroyers or analog warships.<ref name="SOTG07" />

[[File:Nebula-classStarDestroyer-SotGSE.png|thumb|left|200px|A ''Nebula''-class Star Destroyer]]
The armament of the ''Nebula''-class Star Destroyer consisted of 40 heavy [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, 40 turbolaser cannons, 20 [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], 8 [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors, 8 [[assault concussion missile]] tubes, each equipped with 30 missiles, and pulse cannons of the size of turbolasers that gave a huge firepower.<ref name="CTD" />
Despite the touting of far superior firepower over the [[Republic-class Star Destroyer|''Republic''-class]], the two had nearly similar armaments: the difference was eight concussion missile launchers for the ''Nebula'', and two additional tractor beams for the ''Republic''. The power generation and model of guns was different enough to make the ''Nebula'' a far superior design to the ''Republic''. In addition, the [[Hull/Legends|hull]] armor and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] systems of the ''Nebula'' outclassed most other ships in the galaxy, including the ''Republic''.<ref name="CTD" />

The ships could carry up to sixty [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], normally including one assault wing made up of a mixture of [[BTL-S8 K-wing assault starfighter|K-wing]] bombers for precision strikes and [[Defender starfighter]] for close support. ''Nebulas'' could also host a space superiority wing consisting of hyperdrive-equipped [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wings]], [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wings]], or upgraded [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]]. They also carried 1,600 troops and enough [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]] for five to six months, depending on configuration.<ref name="CTD" />

''Nebula''-class Star Destroyers were equipped with a Class 1 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] for faster than light travel.<ref name="CTD" /> The design also incorporated a system of lateral thrust vents to increase the ship's maneuverability, giving it far greater agility than most large [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]].<ref name="SOTG'01" />

Following the New Class practice of using the same hull for multiple configurations, the ''Nebula''-class Star Destroyers shared their basic spaceframe design with the [[Endurance-class fleet carrier|''Endurance''-class fleet carriers]].<ref name="CTD" />

==History==
[[File:Dsdsotg.jpg|thumb|left|220px|''Nebula''-class Star Destroyers in space.]]
Development of the ''Nebula''-class Star Destroyer began shortly after the fall of Coruscant. Formally renamed as the Defender when it was made a part of the [[Defender (ship-design program)|Defender design program]], the ''Nebula''-class refined the core elements of [[Walex Blissex/Legends|Walex Blissex's]] ''Republic''-class design into a smaller, more angular hull. The Defender program was derailed by delays caused by political distractions and the campaigns of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and the reborn [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]. When work eventually resumed the design became part of the New Class program and lent its hull design to the ''Endurance''-class carrier.<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> In [[15 ABY/Legends|15 ABY]], the ''Nebula''-class became the last of the eight new starship designs of the New Class to enter service.<ref name="TNEC" /> The ship's introduction was said to be ill-timed, as it occurred during what was considered to be the most peaceful year since the founding of the New Republic.<ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref>

The first of the class to enter service was the [[Obi-Wan (Nebula-class)|''Obi-Wan'']], named after one of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion's]] heroes, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. The ship was commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Whyrrryk]], a female [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]].<ref name="SOTG'01" /> Due to the process of building compact yet powerful warships of this sort, only a small number of ''Nebula''-class ships were built. Only ten were in service c. [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], with projections of one more being launched each year until the New Class build program was completed.<ref name="CTD" /> When the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]], the few ships of the New Republic's valuable ''Nebula''-class were kept close to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] in reserve fleets due to political pressure. Despite this, ''Nebula''s performed well in clashes with the extra-galactic warships once they finally did see combat. Even considering its age, the ''Nebula''-class still represented the peak of modern, efficient Star Destroyer design during the Yuuzhan Vong War.<ref name="SOTG07" />

At least one ''Defender''-class Star Destroyer was still in service by the time of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]]. [[Pol Temm]] maintained the ship as part of his [[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]] defense fleet. Attempting to enforce his no-weapons rule, he entered a space battle with a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] faction called the [[Mandalorian Supremacists]] and was reportedly killed when his ''Defender'' blew up.<ref name="VSV" />

==Behind the scenes==
Official [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] information on this ship class has a long and twisted history. [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]], the author of the [[Black Fleet Crisis]] novels, called the Star Destroyer twin for the ''Endurance''-class carrier the "''Nebula''-class", but the RPG sourcebook ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' named this ship the ''Defender''-class Star Destroyer, and gave it a complicated in-universe history as part of a "Defender" project, along with the [[Defender-class assault carrier|''Defender''-class assault carrier]] (another "New Class" design created by Kube-McDowell) and the [[Defender starfighter]] (an older WEG ship based on a toy from the animated series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']]).<ref name="CTD" />

The sourcebook also included the only official images (at the time) of the ''Nebula''-class and ''Endurance''-class. Unlike the pictures of the other New Class ships in the book, these two ship classes actually looked like they could have been built on the same spaceframe. This meant that they could not be dismissed as easily as the other pictures of New Class ships, although it was hard to see how the ''Endurance''-class matched the description of [[Intrepid (Endurance-class)|''Intrepid'']] as "fat-hulled" in ''[[Before the Storm]]''.

[[File:Dsdctd.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Defender''-class Star Destroyer as depicted in ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''.]]
In a later RPG sourcebook, ''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'', the ''Defender''-class appeared once again, but with slightly changed statistics: the ship's consumables were increased from 5 to 6 months, and it now carried 60 "starfighters and shuttles" rather than the single wing of the earlier resource.<ref name="SOTG'01" /> The [[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|Saga Edition]] of the same sourcebook would eventually increase the starfighter compliment still further to 60 starfighters and 12 "space transports".<ref name="SOTG07" /> ''Starships of the Galaxy'' also had a new picture of the ''Defender'', which was sleek, dagger-shaped, and far more similar to other Star Destroyer designs.<ref name="SOTG'01" />

''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' added more confusion to the debate by saying that the official name is the ''Nebula''-class Star Destroyer, retconning its name to the scheme originally set down by Michael P. Kube-McDowell.

[[2011]]'s [[Invasion: Revelations 3|''Invasion: Revelations'' 3]] saw the appearance of a pair of ships specifically called ''Defender''-class, using the appearance from Cracken's Threat Dossier, possibly making the ''Nebula'' and ''Defender'' into two entirely separate classes of Star Destroyer.

[[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' would seek to tie up many of the loose ends regarding the New Class program in a section author [[Jason Fry]] jokingly considered calling "continuity hell".<ref name="endnotes 14">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/26352866819/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-14|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 14}}</ref> It established that the ''Nebula''-class was formally renamed Defender (with no use of "class" or italics) when it was made part of the Defender program. That program stalled and when development on the ship restarted as part of the New Class program several years later it was seemingly back under the original ''Nebula'' name.

It is interesting to note that the ''Nebula''-class Star Destroyer and ''Endurance''-class fleet carrier closely mimic the scheme of the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] and [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In both cases, the two destroyers are on the same level of power, but one serves more of a ship-to-ship combat role while the other is more of an escort carrier.
